mnemonics.title: ORACLE Types
mnemonics.content: S.H.I.N.O.—Software, Hardware, Inbound, iNbound, Outbound: Wait, better: 'SHE' = Software, Hardware, External (Human). Remember: S.H.I.N. (Software, Hardware, Inbound/Outbound, iNbound/Outbound) - no, simpler: 'Software, Hardware, Human, Inbound, Outbound' = 5 main oracle categories.

mnemonics.title: Oracle Security Risks
mnemonics.content: M.A.N.S.S.—Manipulation, Aggregation failure, No single point (lack of), Stale data, Sybil attacks. Or: ORACLE problems = Oracle Manipulation, Centralization Risk, Data Freshness, Collusion.

mnemonics.title: Smart Oracle Functions
mnemonics.content: F.E.T.C.H.—Fetch external data, Execute logic, Transform/validate, Cryptographically sign, Handle multiple sources.

acronyms.usage: Proves data authenticity from web servers without revealing secret keys using TLS protocol splitting
Chainlink's feature providing cryptographically provable randomness for gaming, NFTs, and fair selection
Contract between oracle providers and users specifying data quality, update frequency, and uptime requirements
Software oracles fetch data from external APIs (weather, prices, sports) to feed smart contracts

visualTips.visualization: Draw blockchain as an island (isolated). Smart Oracles as bridges connecting the island to the mainland (external world). Show data 'vehicles' crossing the bridge in both directions.
Show a star topology: Smart contract at center, multiple oracle nodes (circles) surrounding it, each connected to different data sources (clouds). Show consensus 'meeting' where all report to center.
Draw flowchart: External Data Source → Oracle Node → Data Aggregation → Smart Contract. Show validation checkpoints at each stage with '✓' marks.

analogies.analogy: Like a webcam streaming live video to your computer, oracles 'stream' live external data (prices, weather) into the blockchain. Without it, the blockchain is 'blind' to the outside world.
Oracles are like translators at an international summit—blockchains speak their own 'language' (code), external APIs speak another (JSON/HTTP). Oracles translate between them.

Centralized oracle = asking one friend for restaurant recommendations (single opinion, could be wrong). Decentralized oracle = asking 10 friends and going with majority vote (more reliable through consensus).
